```{r echo = F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6, fig4d}
axis(2, at = 0:3, tick = F, las = 1, line = -0.05)

lines(x=c(0,0),
      y=c(0,3.5),
      lwd = 3,
      xpd = T)

lines(x=c(0,27),
      y=c(0,0),
      lwd = 3,
      xpd = T)

lines(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
      y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[1],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
      y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[2],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'day'],
      y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[5],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'day'],
      y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[6],
      lwd = 2)

points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)


points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[1], .5),
       col = pcol[1],
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)

points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[2], .5),
       col = pcol[2],
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[5], .5),
       col = pcol[5],
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[6], .5),
       col = pcol[6],
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)

legend("topright",
       legend = c('Silent', 'A4V',
                  'OK371>gbb;Silent', 'OK371>gbb;A4V'),
       pch = c(23,21,23,21,23,21),
       pt.bg = c(hex(pcol[1], .5), hex(pcol[2], .5), hex(pcol[5], .5), hex(pcol[6], .5)),
       bty = 'n',
       y.intersp = 1.2,
       pt.cex = 2,
       xpd=TRUE)

```

```{r echo = F, include = F}

mokgbb$geno = factor(mokgbb$geno, levels = c("silent", "silentok", "A4V", "A4Vok"))

mokgbb$vial_ID = paste0(mokgbb$geno, substr(mokgbb$vial_ID, nchar(mokgbb$vial_ID), nchar(mokgbb$vial_ID)))

fit <- lmer(
  corrected.values ~ geno * day +
    (1 | vial_ID),
  data = mokgbb
)

anova(fit)
print("anova results")
print("geno-There is a significant difference between genotypes")
print("day-climbing changes over time")
print("geno:day-There is  a significant difference in how genotypes change over time")

emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ geno)
pairs(emm, adjust = "holm")
print("calculates model estimated means of each genotype then compares them with a t-test on model adjusted means with holm multiple testing correction")
print("A4V has significantly lower velocity than each other condition. ")


trends <- emtrends(fit, ~ geno, var = "day")
pairs(trends, adjust = "holm")
print("calculates model estimated slope for each genotype over time then compares them with a t-test using model adjusted means and estimated standard error with a holm multiple testing correction")
##estimates the difference in age associated change in rate
print("All genotype comparisons are significant, but the A4V-OK371>gbb compared to Silent-OK371>gbb and to A4V were the most significant. Important note, since we are comparing slopes and taking the vial in to account as a factor, ")

emtrends(fit, ~ geno, var = "day")
print("The estimated rate of declines (day.trend). The slowest rate of decline is A4Vok, followed by Silent, then Silentok with A4V having the fastest estimated rate of decline. Notably, the SE was distinctly higher in A4V explaining the observed p.value.")
```

**Figure 4D** Climbing plots show the mean at each time point. Climbing rate was fit to a linear mixed-effects model to account for genotype, age, and repeated measurements from the same vial. Model term significance was then analyzed using an ANOVA. There was a significant genotype (p = 1.289e-07), day (p = < 2.2e-16) and the interaction effect (p = 2.112e-07) on climbing rate. Post hoc pairwise analysis of the significant interaction was done using estimated marginal means (emmeans) and comparison of the genotype specific slopes (emtrends). Comparing the genotype specific slopes found that A4V had a significantly faster decrease in activity over time than OK371>gbb, A4V (p = 0.0014), Silent (p = 0.0117), and OK371>gbb, Silent (p = 0.0428). The estimated marginal means were found to be significantly lower for A4V than OK371>gbb, A4V (p = <.0001), Silent (p = <.0001), and OK371>gbb, Silent (<.0001).<br><br>

<a id="fig4e"></a>

## Figure 4E: A4V Female ChAT>SaxA Climbing

```{r echo = F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6, fig4e}

f.mean = subset(fig4cd.mean, sex == "F")
f.mean = f.mean[order(f.mean$day),]

par(mar=c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 15.1))

plot(x = NA,
      y = NA,
     type = 'n',
     ylim = c(0, 2.25),
     xlim = c(1,32),
     ylab = 'Mean Velocity (cm/s)',
     xlab = 'Day',
     xaxt = 'n',
     yaxt = 'n',
     main = "Females",
     frame.plot = F)

axis(1, at = 1:32, label = F, lwd = 3, line = -0.75)
axis(1, at = 2*(1:16), tick = F, line = -0.75)

axis(2, at = 0:2, label = F, lwd = 3, line = -0.2)
axis(2, at = 0:2, tick = F, las = 1, line = -0.2)

lines(x=c(0,0),
      y=c(0,2),
      lwd = 3,
      xpd = T)

lines(x=c(0,32),
      y=c(0,0),
      lwd = 3,
      xpd = T)


lines(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
      y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[1],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
      y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[2],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silentcha', 'day'],
      y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silentcha', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[3],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'day'],
      y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[4],
      lwd = 2)

points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)
points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silentcha', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silentcha', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)

points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[1], .5),
       col = pcol[1],
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)

points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[2], .5),
       col = pcol[2],
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)
points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silentcha', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silentcha', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[3], .5),
       col = pcol[3],
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[4], .5),
       col = pcol[4],
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)

legend("topright",
       legend = c('Silent', 'A4V',
                  'ChAT>saxA;Silent', 'ChAT>saxA;A4V'),
       pch = c(23,21,23,21,23,21),
       pt.bg = c(hex(pcol[1], .5), hex(pcol[2], .5), hex(pcol[3], .5), hex(pcol[4], .5)),
       bty = 'n',
       y.intersp = 1.2,
       pt.cex = 2,
       xpd=TRUE)

```

**Figure 4E** Climbing plots show the mean at each time point. Climbing rate was fit to a linear mixed-effects model to account for genotype, age, and repeated measurements from the same vial. Model term significance was then analyzed using an ANOVA. There was a significant genotype (p = < 2.2e-16), day (p = < 2.2e-16) and the interaction effect (p = 2.112e-07) on climbing rate. Post hoc pairwise analysis of the significant interaction was done using estimated marginal means (emmeans) and comparison of the genotype specific slopes (emtrends). Comparing the genotype specific slopes found that A4V had a significantly slower decrease in activity over time than ChAT>SaxA, A4V (p = <.0001), Silent (p = <.0001), and ChAT>SaxA, Silent (<.0001). The estimated marginal means were found to be significantly lower for A4V than ChAT>SaxA, A4V (p = <.0001), Silent (p = <.0001), and ChAT>SaxA, Silent (<.0001). <a href="https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1DFGjCQngjJ6EP_f6i5D3gnMSfJ2tiLoK_QyIkrhGc2s/edit?usp=sharing" target="_blank">Fig4EFGH Data</a><br><br>

```{r echo = F, include = F}

fchasax$geno = factor(fchasax$geno, levels = c("silent", "silentcha", "A4V", "A4Vcha"))

fchasax$vial_ID = paste0(fchasax$geno, substr(fchasax$vial_ID, nchar(fchasax$vial_ID), nchar(fchasax$vial_ID)))

fit <- lmer(
  corrected.values ~ geno * day +
    (1 | vial_ID),
  data = fchasax
)

anova(fit)
print("anova results")
print("geno-There is a significant difference between genotypes")
print("day-climbing changes over time")
print("geno:day-There is  a significant difference in how genotypes change over time")

emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ geno)
pairs(emm, adjust = "holm")
print("calculates model estimated means of each genotype then compares them with a t-test on model adjusted means with holm multiple testing correction")
print("A4V has significantly lower velocity than each other condition. ")


trends <- emtrends(fit, ~ geno, var = "day")
pairs(trends, adjust = "holm")
print("calculates model estimated slope for each genotype over time then compares them with a t-test using model adjusted means and estimated standard error with a holm multiple testing correction")
##estimates the difference in age associated change in rate
print("A4V has a significantly lower rate of decline than all other genotypes.")

emtrends(fit, ~ geno, var = "day")
print("The estimated rate of declines (day.trend). The slowest rate of decline by far is is A4V.")
```

<a id="fig4f"></a>

## Figure 4F: A4V Female OK371>SaxA Climbing

```{r echo = F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6, fig4f}

f.mean = subset(fig4cd.mean, sex == "F")
f.mean = f.mean[order(f.mean$day),]

par(mar=c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 15.1))

plot(x = NA,
      y = NA,
     type = 'n',
     ylim = c(0, 2.25),
     xlim = c(1,32),
     ylab = 'Mean Velocity (cm/s)',
     xlab = 'Day',
     xaxt = 'n',
     yaxt = 'n',
     main = "Females",
     frame.plot = F)

axis(1, at = 1:32, label = F, lwd = 3, line = -0.75)
axis(1, at = 2*(1:16), tick = F, line = -0.75)

axis(2, at = 0:2, label = F, lwd = 3, line = -0.2)
axis(2, at = 0:2, tick = F, las = 1, line = -0.2)

lines(x=c(0,0),
      y=c(0,2),
      lwd = 3,
      xpd = T)

lines(x=c(0,32),
      y=c(0,0),
      lwd = 3,
      xpd = T)

lines(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
      y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[1],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
      y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[2],
      lwd = 2)



lines(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'day'],
      y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[5],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'day'],
      y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[6],
      lwd = 2)

points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)

points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)

points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[1], .5),
       col = pcol[1],
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)

points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[2], .5),
       col = pcol[2],
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)

points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[5], .5),
       col = pcol[5],
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'day'],
       y = f.mean[f.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[6], .5),
       col = pcol[6],
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)

legend("topright",
       legend = c('Silent', 'A4V',
                  'OK371>saxA;Silent', 'OK371>saxA;A4V'),
       pch = c(23,21,23,21,23,21, 26, 32),
       pt.bg = c(hex(pcol[1], .5), hex(pcol[2], .5), hex(pcol[5], .5), hex(pcol[6], .5)),
       bty = 'n',
       y.intersp = 1.2,
       pt.cex = 2,
       xpd=TRUE)

```

**Figure 4F** Climbing plots show the mean at each time point. Climbing rate was fit to a linear mixed-effects model to account for genotype, age, and repeated measurements from the same vial. Model term significance was then analyzed using an ANOVA. There was a significant genotype (p = < 2.2e-16), day (p = < 2.2e-16) and the interaction effect (p = 4.809e-11) on climbing rate. Post hoc pairwise analysis of the significant interaction was done using estimated marginal means (emmeans) and comparison of the genotype specific slopes (emtrends). Comparing the genotype specific slopes found that A4V had a significantly slower decrease in activity over time than OK371>SaxA, A4V (p = <.0001), Silent (p = <.0001), and OK371>SaxA, Silent (p <.0001). The estimated marginal means were found to be significantly lower for A4V than OK371>SaxA, A4V (p = <.0001), Silent (p = <.0001), and OK371>SaxA, Silent (<.0001).<br><br>

```{r echo = F, include = F}

foksax$geno = factor(foksax$geno, levels = c("silent", "silentok", "A4V", "A4Vok"))

foksax$vial_ID = paste0(foksax$geno, substr(foksax$vial_ID, nchar(foksax$vial_ID), nchar(foksax$vial_ID)))

fit <- lmer(
  corrected.values ~ geno * day +
    (1 | vial_ID),
  data = foksax
)

anova(fit)
print("anova results")
print("geno-There is a significant difference between genotypes")
print("day-climbing changes over time")
print("geno:day-There is  a significant difference in how genotypes change over time")

emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ geno)
pairs(emm, adjust = "holm")
print("calculates model estimated means of each genotype then compares them with a t-test on model adjusted means with holm multiple testing correction")
print("A4V has significantly lower velocity than each other condition. ")


trends <- emtrends(fit, ~ geno, var = "day")
pairs(trends, adjust = "holm")
print("calculates model estimated slope for each genotype over time then compares them with a t-test using model adjusted means and estimated standard error with a holm multiple testing correction")
##estimates the difference in age associated change in rate
print("A4V has a significantly lower rate of decline than all other genotypes.")

emtrends(fit, ~ geno, var = "day")
print("The estimated rate of declines (day.trend). The slowest rate of decline by far is is A4V.")
```

<a id="fig4g"></a>

## Figure 4G: A4V Male ChAT>SaxA Climbing

```{r echo = F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6, fig4g}

m.mean = subset(fig4cd.mean, sex == "M")
m.mean = m.mean[order(m.mean$day),]

par(mar=c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 15.1))

plot(x = NA,
      y = NA,
     type = 'n',
     ylim = c(0, 2.25),
     xlim = c(1,32),
     ylab = 'Mean Velocity (cm/s)',
     xlab = 'Day',
     xaxt = 'n',
     yaxt = 'n',
     main = "Males",
     frame.plot = F)

axis(1, at = 1:32, label = F, lwd = 3, line = -0.75)
axis(1, at = 2*(1:16), tick = F, line = -0.75)

axis(2, at = 0:2, label = F, lwd = 3, line = -0.2)
axis(2, at = 0:2, tick = F, las = 1, line = -0.2)

lines(x=c(0,0),
      y=c(0,2),
      lwd = 3,
      xpd = T)

lines(x=c(0,32),
      y=c(0,0),
      lwd = 3,
      xpd = T)

lines(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
      y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = lcol[1],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
      y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[2],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentcha', 'day'],
      y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentcha', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[3],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'day'],
      y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[4],
      lwd = 2)

points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentcha', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentcha', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)

points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[1], .5),
       col = pcol[1],
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)

points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[2], .5),
       col = pcol[2],
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentcha', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentcha', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[3], .5),
       col = pcol[3],
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[4], .5),
       col = pcol[4],
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)

legend("topright",
       legend = c('Silent', 'A4V',
                  'ChAT>saxA;Silent', 'ChAT>saxA;A4V'),
       pch = c(23,21,23,21,23,21, 26, 32),
       pt.bg = c(hex(pcol[1], .5), hex(pcol[2], .5), hex(pcol[3], .5),
                hex(pcol[4], .5)),
       bty = 'n',
       y.intersp = 1.2,
       pt.cex = 2,
       xpd=TRUE)

```

```{r echo = F, include = F}

mchasax$geno = factor(mchasax$geno, levels = c("silent", "silentcha", "A4V", "A4Vcha"))

mchasax$vial_ID = paste0(mchasax$geno, substr(mchasax$vial_ID, nchar(mchasax$vial_ID), nchar(mchasax$vial_ID)))

fit <- lmer(
  corrected.values ~ geno * day +
    (1 | vial_ID),
  data = mchasax
)

anova(fit)
print("anova results")
print("geno-There is a significant difference between genotypes")
print("day-climbing changes over time")
print("geno:day-There is  a significant difference in how genotypes change over time")

emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ geno)
pairs(emm, adjust = "holm")
print("calculates model estimated means of each genotype then compares them with a t-test on model adjusted means with holm multiple testing correction")
print("A4V has significantly lower velocity than each other condition. ")


trends <- emtrends(fit, ~ geno, var = "day")
pairs(trends, adjust = "holm")
print("calculates model estimated slope for each genotype over time then compares them with a t-test using model adjusted means and estimated standard error with a holm multiple testing correction")
##estimates the difference in age associated change in rate
print("A4V has a significantly higher rate of decline than all other genotypes.")

emtrends(fit, ~ geno, var = "day")
print("The estimated rate of declines (day.trend). The highest rate of decline by far is A4V which is almost double that of all other genotypes even though it's only 2 days.")
```

**Figure 4G** Climbing plots show the mean at each time point. Climbing rate was fit to a linear mixed-effects model to account for genotype, age, and repeated measurements from the same vial. Model term significance was then analyzed using an ANOVA. There was a significant genotype (p = < 0.0001079), day (p = < 2.2e-16) and the interaction effect (p = 0.0074109) on climbing rate. Post hoc pairwise analysis of the significant interaction was done using estimated marginal means (emmeans) and comparison of the genotype specific slopes (emtrends). Comparing the genotype specific slopes found that A4V had a significantly slower decrease in activity over time than ChAT>SaxA, A4V (p = 0.0189), Silent (p = 0.0082), and ChAT>SaxA, Silent ( p = 0.0069). The estimated marginal means were found to be significantly lower for A4V than ChAT>SaxA, A4V (p = <.0001), Silent (p = <.0001), and ChAT>SaxA, Silent (<.0001).<br><br>

<a id="fig4h"></a>

## Figure 4H: A4V Male OK371>SaxA Climbing

```{r echo = F, fig.width=10, fig.height=6, fig4h}

m.mean = subset(fig4cd.mean, sex == "M")
m.mean = m.mean[order(m.mean$day),]

par(mar=c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 15.1))

plot(x = NA,
      y = NA,
     type = 'n',
     ylim = c(0, 2.25),
     xlim = c(1,32),
     ylab = 'Mean Velocity (cm/s)',
     xlab = 'Day',
     xaxt = 'n',
     yaxt = 'n',
     main = "Males",
     frame.plot = F)

axis(1, at = 1:32, label = F, lwd = 3, line = -0.75)
axis(1, at = 2*(1:16), tick = F, line = -0.75)

axis(2, at = 0:2, label = F, lwd = 3, line = -0.2)
axis(2, at = 0:2, tick = F, las = 1, line = -0.2)

lines(x=c(0,0),
      y=c(0,2),
      lwd = 3,
      xpd = T)

lines(x=c(0,32),
      y=c(0,0),
      lwd = 3,
      xpd = T)

lines(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
      y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = lcol[1],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
      y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[2],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'day'],
      y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[5],
      lwd = 2)

lines(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'day'],
      y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'mean'],
      type = 'l',
      col = pcol[6],
      lwd = 2)

points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vcha', 'mean'],
       bg = 'white',
       col = 'white',
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)

points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silent', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[1], .5),
       col = pcol[1],
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)

points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4V', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[2], .5),
       col = pcol[2],
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'silentok', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[5], .5),
       col = pcol[5],
       pch = 23,
       cex = 1)
points(x = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'day'],
       y = m.mean[m.mean$geno == 'A4Vok', 'mean'],
       bg = hex(pcol[6], .5),
       col = pcol[6],
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1)

legend("topright",
       legend = c('Silent', 'A4V',
                  'OK371>saxA;Silent', 'OK371>saxA;A4V'),
       pch = c(23,21,23,21,23,21, 26, 32),
       pt.bg = c(hex(pcol[1], .5), hex(pcol[2], .5), hex(pcol[5], .5),
                hex(pcol[6], .5)),
       bty = 'n',
       y.intersp = 1.2,
       pt.cex = 2,
       xpd=TRUE)

```

```{r echo = F, include = F}

moksax$geno = factor(moksax$geno, levels = c("silent", "silentok", "A4V", "A4Vok"))

moksax$vial_ID = paste0(moksax$geno, substr(moksax$vial_ID, nchar(moksax$vial_ID), nchar(moksax$vial_ID)))

fit <- lmer(
  corrected.values ~ geno * day +
    (1 | vial_ID),
  data = moksax
)

anova(fit)
print("anova results")
print("geno-There is a significant difference between genotypes")
print("day-climbing changes over time")
print("geno:day-There is  a significant difference in how genotypes change over time")

emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ geno)
pairs(emm, adjust = "holm")
print("calculates model estimated means of each genotype then compares them with a t-test on model adjusted means with holm multiple testing correction")
print("A4V has significantly lower velocity than each other condition. ")


trends <- emtrends(fit, ~ geno, var = "day")
pairs(trends, adjust = "holm")
print("calculates model estimated slope for each genotype over time then compares them with a t-test using model adjusted means and estimated standard error with a holm multiple testing correction")
##estimates the difference in age associated change in rate
print("A4V has a significantly higher rate of decline than all other genotypes. All comparisons were again significant.")

emtrends(fit, ~ geno, var = "day")
print("The estimated rate of declines (day.trend). The fastest rate of decline by far is is A4V, then silentok, then silent and A4Vok has the slowest rate of decline.")
```

**Figure 4H** Climbing plots show the mean at each time point. Climbing rate was fit to a linear mixed-effects model to account for genotype, age, and repeated measurements from the same vial. Model term significance was then analyzed using an ANOVA. There was a significant genotype (p = < 1.289e-07), day (p = < 2.2e-16) and the interaction effect (p = 2.112e-07) on climbing rate. Post hoc pairwise analysis of the significant interaction was done using estimated marginal means (emmeans) and comparison of the genotype specific slopes (emtrends). Comparing the genotype specific slopes found that A4V had a significantly slower decrease in activity over time than OK371>SaxA, A4V (p = 0.0014), Silent (p = 0.0117), and OK371>SaxA, Silent (p = 0.0428). The estimated marginal means were found to be significantly lower for A4V than OK371>SaxA, A4V (p = <.0001), Silent (p = <.0001), and OK371>SaxA, Silent (<.0001).
